---
title: "Aged-TBI-RNAseq-Analysis"
author: "Milana Serkin"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## DESeq2

```{r knitr}
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir ="/Users/milana/Desktop")
```

Loading all of the libraries

```{r Loading Libraries}

lst = c("dplyr", "ggplot2", "readr", "stringr", "RColorBrewer", "DESeq2", "EnhancedVolcano", "RColorBrewer", "pheatmap", "forestplot")

load_libraries = function(list_of_packages){
  for (i in lst) {
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(i, character.only = TRUE))}
}

load_libraries(lst)
```

## Loading in the data: both counts and metatable

```{r Loading in the data}

library(tidyverse)

#Reading in csv file
#Data from: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE206932
#Paper for comparison: https://elifesciences.org/articles/81154#content

counts <- read_csv("GSE206932_merged.counts.bulk.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) 
  colnames(counts)[1] <- "gene_id"
  counts <- counts %>%
  mutate(across(-gene_id, ~ as.integer(round(.x)))) %>%
  column_to_rownames("gene_id")

head(counts)

#Confirm correct dimensions
# 1. Check the exact number of rows and columns
dim(counts) 
# Expected output: [1] 55385    12
# 2. Verify the column names (Should list your 12 sample names)
colnames(counts)
# 3. Peek at the bottom of the dataset to ensure it didn't cut off
tail(counts)
```

```{r Making Metadata}
#Make a metadata table for this dataset

# 1. Extract sample IDs (excluding the first column)
#sample_ids <- colnames(counts)[-1]
#Changing this because gene_id = row names
sample_ids <- colnames(counts)

# 2. Parse sample IDs
parsed_ids <- strsplit(sample_ids, "_") #not used

metadata <- data.frame(
  Sample = sample_ids,

  # Condition 1 
  Age = ifelse(substr(sample_ids, 1, 1) == "Y", "Young", "Aged"),

  # Condition 2 
  Condition = ifelse(substr(sample_ids, 2, 2) == "S", "Sham", "TBI"),
  
  #Condition 3 
  Replicate = paste0("Rep_", substr(sample_ids, 3, 3))
)

# 4. Set columns to factors
rownames(metadata) <- sample_ids
metadata$Age <- factor(metadata$Age, levels = c("Young", "Aged"))
metadata$Condition <- factor(metadata$Condition, levels = c("Sham", "TBI"))
metadata$Replicate <- factor(metadata$Replicate)

print(metadata)
```

## 

## DESeq2 analysis

```{r DESeq2 setup}
get_dds_all = function(metadata,counts){
  columnData <- metadata
  
  keep <- rowSums(counts) >= 10
  countData <- counts[keep, ]
  
  dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = countData, colData = metadata, design = ~ Age * Condition)

  dds <- DESeq(dds)   
  
  return(dds)
}

print_deseqTable = function(dds){
  res <- results(dds, contrast = c("Condition", "TBI", "Sham"))
  outfile <- paste0("/Users/milana/Desktop/deseq-results2.txt")
  write.table(res, file = outfile, sep = '\t', quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
  return(res)
}
```

```{r DESeq2 analysis}

#perform deseq
dds_all = get_dds_all(metadata,counts)
# Remove the "gene:" prefix from the "Gene" column
rownames(dds_all) <- gsub("gene:", "", rownames(dds_all))

deseq_normalized_counts <- as.data.frame(counts(dds_all, normalized = TRUE))

deseq_normalized_counts$Gene_id <- row.names(deseq_normalized_counts)

deseq_normalized_counts <- deseq_normalized_counts[, c(ncol(deseq_normalized_counts), 1:(ncol(deseq_normalized_counts)-1))]

#write.table(deseq_normalized_counts, "/Users/milana/Desktop/deseq_norm_counts2.tsv", sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)

print_deseqTable(dds_all)
```

## PCA

```{r PCA}
get_pca = function(dds, title) {
  
  trans_data <- rlog(dds, blind = FALSE) 
  
  # Use DESeq2's built in PCA calculator
  pca_data <- plotPCA(trans_data, intgroup = c("Age", "Condition"), returnData = TRUE)
  
  # Extract the percent variance calculated by DESeq2
  percent_var <- round(100 * attr(pca_data, "percentVar"))
  
  # DESeq2 glues the variables together with a colon (:) 
  custom_colors <- c("Young:Sham" = "#1B9E77", "Young:TBI" = "#bf60a5ff", 
                     "Aged:Sham" = "#7570B3", "Aged:TBI" = "#377eb8")
  
  # Change color = c("Age", "Condition") to color = group
  ggplot(pca_data, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = group, label = name)) +
    geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.8) +  
    coord_fixed() +
    scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = paste0("PC1: ", percent_var[1], "% variance"),
      y = paste0("PC2: ", percent_var[2], "% variance"),
      color = "Experimental Group" 
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face="bold"))
}

# Run it
pca_plot = get_pca(dds_all, "PCA: 4 Experimental Groups")
print(pca_plot)

```

```         
```

## Get DE genes and Volcanoes

```{r DE genes and Volcanoes}
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)

padj_cutoff <- 0.1
lfc_cutoff <- 0

# 1. Function to run a fresh DESeq2 model on a subset of data
run_pairwise_deseq <- function(meta_subset, counts_full, factor_name, target_lvl, ref_lvl) {
  
  # A. Subset the counts to match the metadata exactly
  counts_sub <- counts_full[, rownames(meta_subset)]
  
  # B. Filter low counts ONLY within these 6 samples
  keep <- rowSums(counts_sub) >= 10
  counts_sub <- counts_sub[keep, ]
  
  # C. Set the reference level (Baseline)
  meta_subset[[factor_name]] <- factor(meta_subset[[factor_name]], levels = c(ref_lvl, target_lvl))
  
  # D. Run a fresh DESeq2 model
  dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts_sub, colData = meta_subset, design = as.formula(paste0("~ ", factor_name)))
  dds <- DESeq(dds)
  
  # E. Get results
  res <- results(dds, contrast = c(factor_name, target_lvl, ref_lvl), alpha = padj_cutoff)
  res_df <- as.data.frame(res)
  res_df <- res_df[!is.na(res_df$padj), ] # Remove NAs
  
  # F. Print counts
  up <- sum(res_df$padj < padj_cutoff & res_df$log2FoldChange > lfc_cutoff)
  down <- sum(res_df$padj < padj_cutoff & res_df$log2FoldChange < -lfc_cutoff)
  
  print(paste("---", target_lvl, "vs", ref_lvl, "---"))
  print(paste0("Up: ", up, " | Down: ", down))
  
  return(res_df)
}

# 2. Volcano Plot Function
volcano_plot <- function(deg_results_df, plot_title) {
  cols <- c("DOWN" = "blue", "NO" = "grey", "UP" = "red")
  
  plot_df <- deg_results_df
  plot_df$diffexpressed <- "NO"
  plot_df$diffexpressed[plot_df$log2FoldChange > lfc_cutoff & plot_df$padj < padj_cutoff] <- "UP"
  plot_df$diffexpressed[plot_df$log2FoldChange < -lfc_cutoff & plot_df$padj < padj_cutoff] <- "DOWN"
  
  up_count <- sum(plot_df$diffexpressed == "UP")
  down_count <- sum(plot_df$diffexpressed == "DOWN")

  x_max <- max(abs(plot_df$log2FoldChange), na.rm = TRUE)
  y_max <- max(-log10(plot_df$padj), na.rm = TRUE)

  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj), col = diffexpressed)) +
    geom_point(size = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
    scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, col = "gray", linetype = 'dashed') +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(padj_cutoff), col = "black", linetype = 'dashed') +
    annotate("text", x = -x_max, y = y_max, label = down_count, 
             color = cols["DOWN"], fontface = "bold", hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 5) +
    annotate("text", x = x_max, y = y_max, label = up_count, 
             color = cols["UP"], fontface = "bold", hjust = 1, vjust = 1, size = 5) +
    labs(title = plot_title, x = "log2 Fold Change", y = "-log10 FDR") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"), legend.position = "none")
  
  return(p)
}
```

```{r Comparisons}

# --- Comparison 1: Young Sham vs Young TBI ---
# Target: 13 Up, 9 Down
meta_young <- metadata[metadata$Age == "Young", ]
res_young <- run_pairwise_deseq(meta_young, counts, factor_name = "Condition", target_lvl = "TBI", ref_lvl = "Sham")
volc1 <- volcano_plot(res_young, "Young Sham vs Young TBI")
print(volc1)

# --- Comparison 2: Aged Sham vs Aged TBI ---
# Target: 41 Up, 323 Down
meta_aged <- metadata[metadata$Age == "Aged", ]
res_aged <- run_pairwise_deseq(meta_aged, counts, factor_name = "Condition", target_lvl = "TBI", ref_lvl = "Sham")
volc2 <- volcano_plot(res_aged, "Aged Sham vs Aged TBI")
print(volc2)

# --- Comparison 3: Young Sham vs Aged Sham ---
# Target: 893 Up, 879 Down
meta_sham <- metadata[metadata$Condition == "Sham", ]
res_sham <- run_pairwise_deseq(meta_sham, counts, factor_name = "Age", target_lvl = "Aged", ref_lvl = "Young")
volc3 <- volcano_plot(res_sham, "Young Sham vs Aged Sham")
print(volc3)

# --- Comparison 4: Young TBI vs Aged TBI ---
# Target: 1239 Up, 1697 Down
meta_tbi <- metadata[metadata$Condition == "TBI", ]
res_tbi <- run_pairwise_deseq(meta_tbi, counts, factor_name = "Age", target_lvl = "Aged", ref_lvl = "Young")
volc4 <- volcano_plot(res_tbi, "Young TBI vs Aged TBI")
print(volc4)
```

```         
```

# Pathway Analysis

```{r pathway analysis}

# PATHWAY ANALYSIS (GAGE & PATHVIEW)


# 1. Load the libraries
library(gage)
library(gageData)
library(pathview)
library(org.Mm.eg.db) # Mouse database for gene ID conversion

# 2. Prepare the Fold Changes

foldchanges <- res_aged$log2FoldChange
names(foldchanges) <- rownames(res_aged)

# 3. Convert Gene Symbols to Entrez IDs
# Pathview needs numeric Entrez IDs (e.g., 14085) instead of symbols (e.g., "Gpx6")
entrez_ids <- mapIds(org.Mm.eg.db,
                     keys = names(foldchanges),
                     column = "ENTREZID",
                     keytype = "SYMBOL",
                     multiVals = "first")

# Replace the symbols with Entrez IDs
names(foldchanges) <- entrez_ids

# Remove any genes that didn't successfully map to an Entrez ID to prevent errors
foldchanges <- foldchanges[!is.na(names(foldchanges))]

# 4. Load the Mouse KEGG Pathway Database
data(kegg.sets.mm)
data(sigmet.idx.mm)
kegg.sets.mm <- kegg.sets.mm[sigmet.idx.mm]

# 5. Run the GAGE Pathway Analysis
# This checks if your specific fold changes significantly alter known biological pathways
keggres <- gage(exprs = foldchanges, gsets = kegg.sets.mm, same.dir = TRUE)

# Print the top 5 up-regulated pathways 
cat("\n--- Top 5 Up-Regulated Pathways in Aged TBI ---\n")
print(head(keggres$greater, 5))

# Print the top 5 down-regulated pathways 
cat("\n--- Top 5 Down-Regulated Pathways in Aged TBI ---\n")
print(head(keggres$less, 5))

# 6. Generate the Pathway Image
# Grab the ID of the absolute most significantly down-regulated pathway
top_pathway <- rownames(keggres$less)[1]
top_pathway_id <- substr(top_pathway, 1, 8) # Extracts just the "mmuXXXXX" ID

cat(paste("\nDrawing Pathview for top pathway:", top_pathway_id, "\n"))

pathview(gene.data = foldchanges, 
         pathway.id = top_pathway_id, 
        species = "mmu", 
         limit = list(gene = max(abs(foldchanges)), cpd = 1))
```
